From the articles reviewed, we see extensive information about different bony structures involved in the formation of the middle cranial fossa, particularly highlighting the contribution of the temporal bone. Specifically, Article [article-29118_6] indicates that the middle cranial fossa consists of the sphenoid bone and two temporal bones. Further details indicate that the mandibular fossa itself does not belong to any other option given—such as the maxilla, sphenoid, or another bone—and instead refers directly back to parts contributing to the overall architecture of the temporal bone.
